summary: Successfully implemented the Semantic Links Engine for YCB Content Layer. Created SemanticLinkEngine class that auto-discovers relationships between content entries using embeddings and similarity scoring. Engine leverages existing N5 vector store infrastructure (vectors_v2.db) and computes cosine similarity between content items. Links are classified by strength (very_similar, related, loosely_related) and stored in the entry_links table. All CLI commands are functional and integrated.
learnings: The existing N5 vector store infrastructure works perfectly for content similarity - found 1321 vectors for 63 unique content library items
Cosine similarity using max similarity across all blocks per item provides good quality semantic links - most meaningful connections score >0.9
NumPy float32 values need explicit conversion to Python float() before SQLite storage to avoid BLOB serialization issues
Content library items use source_file_path field (not file_path) for mapping to vector store resources
CLI integration pattern works well - semantic_links.py as standalone tool, n5_content_cli.py as unified interface
Link classification by similarity thresholds (>0.9 very_similar, >0.85 related, >0.75 loosely_related) produces intuitive results
Graph traversal for link visualization works but depth 2 is usually sufficient to show meaningful relationships without overwhelming output
The existing entry_links table schema from D1.1 was perfect for storing computed semantic links - no schema changes needed
